examples: It is the economy that will galvanise them.
Though these bodies were nothing more than talking shops for the elite, they did galvanise the Han gentry into action.
They had been expected to galvanise the summit and steamroller opposition to an agreement, but in fact proved counterproductive.
In today's 50-50 America, Mr Bush's main priority is to galvanise his half of the electorate.
These efforts have had mixed success, but they have served to galvanise public opinion.
